title: Miso Jamaican Lobster Stew
description: A charred, glassy-jelly stew that blends Jamaican heat with Japanese umami, perfect for a lazy Sunday low‑carb dinner.
instructions: Heat a nonstick skillet over medium heat until shimmering.
Place the lobster tails in the skillet, searing each side for 2 minutes until charred and a dark brown crust forms.
Add the garlic, ginger, and Scotch bonnet pepper, sautéing for 1 minute until fragrant.
Stir in the miso paste and shoyu‑koji, coating the lobster and aromatics for another 2 minutes.
Pour in the coconut milk and lime juice, bringing the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Reduce heat to low and add thyme sprigs, letting the stew reduce for 10 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened to a glassy consistency.
Remove thyme and season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve hot, scooping portions over cauliflower rice or steamed greens for a low‑carb finish.
